title: Multi-Tenant Edge Accelerator Allocation
scenario: A cloud service manages factory edge gateways. Each gateway has a single Edge TPU (4 TOPS). It must run Model A (ResNet-18, 10ms execution, needs 30fps) and Model B (YOLOv5s, 30ms execution, needs 10fps). title: Bandwidth-Constrained OTA Pipelines
scenario: You operate a cloud service that pushes weekly updates for an 800MB vision model to 50,000 autonomous delivery robots. Cellular data egress costs $0.10/GB. Sending the full model costs $4,000 per week. You are tasked with reducing OTA pipeline costs by 90% without degrading model performance.

title: Cloud-Edge Federated Learning Memory Limits
scenario: You are designing a cloud orchestration system for 5 million smart home acoustic sensors (Cortex-M33, 256KB SRAM). The cloud manages federated learning to update a 120KB TFLite Micro model. title: Power-Aware Cloud-Edge Fallback

title: Async FL for Intermittent Solar Devices
scenario: You manage 100,000 solar-powered agricultural sensors (100uW harvest rate). The cloud orchestrates FedAvg for a microclimate model. Devices only wake up for 5 seconds every hour. Synchronous FedAvg fails entirely because less than 1% of devices have overlapping wake windows, causing aggregation time

title: Cloud-Managed Federated QAT
scenario: A cloud pipeline aggregates updates from 2 million smartwatches running CMSIS-NN. Inference uses strict INT8 precision to fit the 256KB SRAM. title: OTA Model Patching for Flash Constraints
scenario: The cloud pushes daily model updates to 500k smart meters. The new model is 200KB. The MCU has 512KB of Flash and 256KB of SRAM. The RTOS/firmware occupies 250KB of Flash. scenario: A factory floor has 10,000 Cortex-M4 sensors collecting 16kHz audio and 1kHz 3-axis IMU data. The cloud runs a massive transformer for predictive maintenance. scenario: You are designing an FL system for wildlife tracking collars (1000mAh battery, 3.7V, 2-year lifespan). The cloud orchestrates FL to adapt behavior classification models. scenario: A cloud orchestrator manages federated learning across 100,000 smartwatches (Cortex-M4, 256KB SRAM). Each watch has a 300mAh battery (3.8V). scenario: Your cloud pipeline uses Neural Architecture Search (NAS) to generate keyword spotting models for Cortex-M0+ devices (128KB SRAM). title: Over-the-Air Weight Updates for IoT
scenario: You manage 50,000 remote pipeline anomaly sensors connected via NB-IoT (100 kbps, 500mW Tx/Rx power). The battery is 5000mAh (3V) and must last 5 years. The cloud needs to push weekly updates to the 150KB INT8 TFLite Micro model. Pushing full models over NB-IoT violates the battery life constraints.
